---
title: Introduction to Open Source
description: A beginner's guide to contributing to free software
lang: en
---

## What is open source? {#what-is-open-source}

Open source software is code released under a [free license](https://example.com/glossary/#free-license) that grants anyone the freedom to use, study, modify, and distribute it.

The most widely used license is `MIT`, though `GPL-3.0` provides stronger copyleft protections.

## How does contributing work? {#how-does-contributing-work}

Contributing starts with finding a project that interests you. Most projects use issue trackers to coordinate work and pull requests to propose changes.

### Code review {#code-review}

Code review is the process of examining proposed changes before they are merged. Reviewers check for correctness, style consistency, and potential regressions.
